call.name: Read
call.output:
1‚Üí# HW03 - Strategy Ballworld: Strategy Pattern
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üí## Design Pattern
     4‚Üí
     5‚ÜíThis assignment refactors HW02 from **inheritance-based** behavior to
     6‚Üí**composition-based** behavior using the **Strategy Pattern**. Instead of
     7‚Üícreating a new ball subclass for each behavior, all balls are instances of the
     8‚Üísame `Ball` class. Each `Ball` holds an `IUpdateStrategy` reference that defines
     9‚Üíits unique behavior. Strategies can be swapped at runtime (via `SwitcherStrategy`)
    10‚Üíand composed together (via combining factories).
    11‚Üí
    12‚Üí## Architecture Overview
    13‚Üí
    14‚Üí```
    15‚ÜíController
    16‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ BallModel (model)
    17‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Timer (50ms)
    18‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ IDispatcher<Graphics> - dispatches to all Ball observers
    19‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ SwitcherStrategy - shared by all "switcher" balls
    20‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ IStrategyFac - factory pattern for creating strategies
    21‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ObjectLoader - loads strategy by class name
    22‚Üí‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ Ball instances (each holds an IUpdateStrategy)
    23‚Üí‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ BallView (view)
    24‚Üí    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Top dropdown - select strategy for new balls
    25‚Üí    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Bottom dropdown - select strategy for combining/switching
    26‚Üí    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ "Make Ball" / "Make Switcher" buttons
    27‚Üí    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ "Combine" / "Switch" buttons
    28‚Üí    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ "Clear" button
    29‚Üí```
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí**Key refactoring from HW02**: The `ABall` abstract class is replaced with a
    32‚Üísingle concrete `Ball` class. Behavior that was in `updateState()` subclass
    33‚Üíoverrides is now in `IUpdateStrategy.updateState(IDispatcher, Ball)` implementations.
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí## What Is Provided
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí- **`Ball.java`** (`src/hw03/model/Ball.java`): Concrete ball with `location`,
    38‚Üí  `velocity`, `color`, `radius`, `dimension`, and `strategy` fields. Methods
    39‚Üí  `move()`, `bounce()`, `paint()`, and `update()` are fully implemented. The
    40‚Üí  `update()` method calls `strategy.updateState(disp, this)`.
    41‚Üí- **`BallModel.java`** (`src/hw03/model/BallModel.java`): Model with strategy
    42‚Üí  factory loading, combining, and switcher management. `makeStrategyFac()`,
    43‚Üí  `combineStrategyFacs()`, `loadBall()`, and `setSwitcher()` are provided.
    44‚Üí- **`IUpdateStrategy.java`** (`src/hw03/model/IUpdateStrategy.java`): Interface
    45‚Üí  with `updateState(IDispatcher<Graphics> disp, Ball ball)`.
    46‚Üí- **`IStrategyFac.java`** (`src/hw03/model/IStrategyFac.java`): Strategy factory
    47‚Üí  interface.
    48‚Üí- **Controller, View, Adapters**: Fully implemented MVC wiring.
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí## What You Implement
    51‚Üí
    52‚ÜíAll strategies are in `src/hw03/model/strategy/` and implement `IUpdateStrategy`.
    53‚ÜíEach strategy's `updateState(IDispatcher<Graphics> disp, Ball ball)` method
    54‚Üímodifies the ball's properties on each tick.
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí### 1. `StraightStrategy.updateState()` -- `src/hw03/model/strategy/StraightStrategy.java`
    57‚Üí
    58‚ÜíA no-op strategy. The ball moves in a straight line using only the default
    59‚Üí`move()` and `bounce()` behavior. The `updateState()` body should be empty.
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üí### 2. `GravityStrategy.updateState()` -- `src/hw03/model/strategy/GravityStrategy.java`
    62‚Üí
    63‚ÜíSimulates gravity by adding a constant value to the ball's y-velocity each tick.
    64‚ÜíGet the ball's velocity and increase the y component by a small constant (e.g., 1):
    65‚Üí
    66‚Üí```java
    67‚Üíball.getVelocity().y += 1;
    68‚Üí```
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí### 3. `ColorStrategy.updateState()` -- `src/hw03/model/strategy/ColorStrategy.java`
    71‚Üí
    72‚ÜíChanges the ball's color to a random color each tick. Use `java.util.Random` or
    73‚Üí`Randomizer.Singleton` to generate a random color and call `ball.setColor(...)`.
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üí### 4. `ReverseStrategy.updateState()` -- `src/hw03/model/strategy/ReverseStrategy.java`
    76‚Üí
    77‚ÜíReverses the ball's velocity every 10 ticks. Use an internal `ticks` counter.
    78‚ÜíWhen `ticks` reaches 10, negate both x and y velocity and reset the counter.
    79‚Üí
    80‚Üí### 5. `ExpandingStrategy.updateState()` -- `src/hw03/model/strategy/ExpandingStrategy.java`
    81‚Üí
    82‚ÜíOscillates the ball's radius between 5 and 60. Use the `expand` boolean field to
    83‚Üítrack direction. When expanding, increment the radius; when contracting, decrement
    84‚Üíit. Flip direction at the bounds (5 and 60).
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üí### 6. `TeleportingStrategy.updateState()` -- `src/hw03/model/strategy/TeleportingStrategy.java`
    87‚Üí
    88‚ÜíEvery 20 ticks, teleport the ball to a random location on the canvas. Use the
    89‚Üí`ticks` counter. When it reaches 20, set the ball's location to a random point
    90‚Üíwithin `ball.getDimension()` bounds and reset the counter.
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üí### 7. `RandomWalkStrategy.updateState()` -- `src/hw03/model/strategy/RandomWalkStrategy.java`
    93‚Üí
    94‚ÜíEach tick, randomly adjust the ball's velocity by a small amount (e.g., +/-1
    95‚Üífor both x and y). This creates a "drunk walk" effect.
    96‚Üí
    97‚Üí### 8. `ExplodingStrategy.updateState()` -- `src/hw03/model/strategy/ExplodingStrategy.java`
    98‚Üí
    99‚ÜíWhen the ball bounces off a wall, it "explodes" by spawning a new ball (added to
   100‚Üíthe dispatcher) and optionally changing direction. Check `ball.hasBounced()` to
   101‚Üídetect wall collisions. Use `disp.addObserver()` to add a new ball.
   102‚Üí
   103‚Üí### 9. `SwitcherStrategy.updateState()` -- `src/hw03/model/strategy/SwitcherStrategy.java`
   104‚Üí
   105‚ÜíDelegates to an internal strategy. The `switchStrategy()` method (provided)
   106‚Üíswaps the internal strategy, and `updateState()` should simply delegate:
   107‚Üí
   108‚Üí```java
   109‚Üístrategy.updateState(disp, ball);
   110‚Üí```
   111‚Üí
   112‚ÜíAll switcher balls share the same `SwitcherStrategy` instance in the model, so
   113‚Üíwhen the internal strategy is switched, all switcher balls change behavior at once.
   114‚Üí
   115‚Üí## File Paths (Editable)
   116‚Üí
   117‚Üí| File | Package | What to implement |
   118‚Üí|------|---------|-------------------|
   119‚Üí| `src/hw03/model/strategy/StraightStrategy.java` | `hw03.model.strategy` | `updateState()` (no-op) |
   120‚Üí| `src/hw03/model/strategy/GravityStrategy.java` | `hw03.model.strategy` | `updateState()` |
   121‚Üí| `src/hw03/model/strategy/ColorStrategy.java` | `hw03.model.strategy` | `updateState()` |
   122‚Üí| `src/hw03/model/strategy/ReverseStrategy.java` | `hw03.model.strategy` | `updateState()` |
   123‚Üí| `src/hw03/model/strategy/ExpandingStrategy.java` | `hw03.model.strategy` | `updateState()` |
   124‚Üí| `src/hw03/model/strategy/TeleportingStrategy.java` | `hw03.model.strategy` | `updateState()` |
   125‚Üí| `src/hw03/model/strategy/RandomWalkStrategy.java` | `hw03.model.strategy` | `updateState()` |
   126‚Üí| `src/hw03/model/strategy/ExplodingStrategy.java` | `hw03.model.strategy` | `updateState()` |
   127‚Üí| `src/hw03/model/strategy/SwitcherStrategy.java` | `hw03.model.strategy` | `updateState()` |
   128‚Üí
   129‚Üí## Expected Behavior
   130‚Üí
   131‚Üí- **Straight**: Ball moves in a straight line, bouncing off walls.
   132‚Üí- **Gravity**: Ball accelerates downward, bouncing with increasing speed.
   133‚Üí- **Color**: Ball continuously changes color randomly.
   134‚Üí- **Reverse**: Ball reverses direction every 10 ticks.
   135‚Üí- **Expanding**: Ball grows and shrinks cyclically between radius 5 and 60.
   136‚Üí- **Teleporting**: Ball jumps to a random location every 20 ticks.
   137‚Üí- **RandomWalk**: Ball moves erratically, changing velocity slightly each tick.
   138‚Üí- **Exploding**: Ball spawns a new ball each time it hits a wall.
   139‚Üí- **Switcher**: All switcher balls delegate to a shared strategy that can be
   140‚Üí  switched at runtime via the GUI dropdown.
   141‚Üí- **Combining**: Two strategies can be composed so a ball exhibits both behaviors.
   142‚Üí
   143‚Üí## Dependency on HW02
   144‚Üí
   145‚ÜíThis assignment builds on HW02 (depends_on: `comp310_hw02`). If available,
   146‚Üí`previous_solution/` contains your HW02 work for reference. Key differences:
   147‚Üí- HW02's `ABall` subclasses are replaced by `Ball` + `IUpdateStrategy`
   148‚Üí- Behavior is now swappable at runtime rather than fixed at compile time
   149‚Üí
   150‚Üí## Testing
   151‚Üí
   152‚ÜíRun the autograder to validate your implementation:
   153‚Üí
   154‚Üí```
   155‚ÜíUse the `grade` tool to run tests.
   156‚Üí```
   157‚Üí
   158‚ÜíThe test suite (`comp310.tests.hw03.StrategyTest`) verifies:
   159‚Üí- Each strategy modifies ball state correctly
   160‚Üí- `GravityStrategy` increases y-velocity
   161‚Üí- `ColorStrategy` changes the ball's color
   162‚Üí- `ReverseStrategy` flips velocity periodically
   163‚Üí- `ExpandingStrategy` oscillates radius within bounds
   164‚Üí- `TeleportingStrategy` relocates the ball periodically
   165‚Üí- `SwitcherStrategy` properly delegates to its internal strategy
   166‚Üí

blocks.text:
Now I have a clear picture. I need to implement the `updateState()` method in all 9 strategy files. Let me also look at the previous solution for reference, particularly the bounced behavior for ExplodingStrategy.

Let me implement all strategies now. The test file gives me the exact expected behavior:
1. StraightStrategy - no-op (already done, empty body)
2. GravityStrategy - `ball.getVelocity().y += 1`
3. ColorStrategy - random color each tick
4. ReverseStrategy - reverse velocity every 10 ticks
5. ExpandingStrategy - oscillate radius between 5 and 60


6. TeleportingStrategy - teleport every 20 ticks
7. RandomWalkStrategy - random velocity adjustment each tick
8. ExplodingStrategy - spawn new ball on bounce
9. SwitcherStrategy - delegate to internal strategy

I'm implementing all of them now based on the test cases. Each strategy has its own timing mechanism‚ÄîGravityStrategy applies constant acceleration, ColorStrategy randomizes on each update, ReverseStrategy tracks ticks to reverse direction at intervals, ExpandingStrategy oscillates the radius between bounds, and TeleportingStrategy repositions at regular intervals.

For the remaining ones, RandomWalkStrategy applies small random velocity tweaks, ExplodingStrategy detects bounces and spawns additional balls, and SwitcherStrategy simply forwards updates to its wrapped strategy. StraightStrategy is already complete as a no-op.
Good, I have a clear picture of all the strategy skeletons. Let me implement all 9 strategies now.

blocks.text:
I have 7 failures. Let me analyze them:

1. **ExpandingStrategy** - expects radius to increase by 2 per tick (not 1), and the boundary behavior is different. "Expanding from radius 10 should yield 12" ‚Äî so it goes up by 2 each tick. "After 5 expansions from radius 10, radius should be 20" ‚Äî 5 * 2 = 10, 10 + 10 = 20. And "At radius 60, should contract to 58" ‚Äî decreases by 2. Also "expected: <60> but was: <59>" suggests the boundary behavior needs to include 60 exactly.


2. **ReverseStrategy** - the reversal needs to happen at the 11th call, so I should change the condition to `ticks > 10` to match when the test expects the velocity to actually flip.
3. **TeleportingStrategy** - the location shouldn't change until after 20 calls, which means I need to check the teleport tick logic.
4. **RandomWalkStrategy** - instead of allowing three possible outcomes, I need to ensure the velocity change is always exactly ¬±1, so I'll use a boolean check to pick between -1 and 1.

Now I'm going through each strategy to apply these fixes.

29/36 passing. I need to fix 4 issues:

1. **ExpandingStrategy**: Tests expect increment/decrement by 2 per tick, not 1
2. **ReverseStrategy**: Reversal should happen on the 11th tick (after 10, not at 10)
3. **TeleportingStrategy**: Teleport on the 21st tick (after 20, not at 20)
4. **RandomWalkStrategy**: Change must be exactly +1 or -1, never 0

blocks.text:
35/36. One more failure: `testExpandingStrategyContractsAtMax` - "At radius 60, should contract to 58". The test sets ball to radius 60 and calls updateState once, expecting 58. But my code sees radius 60, sets `expand = false`, then... wait, it sets `r = 60` and doesn't decrement. The issue is that when the ball is already at 60, the first tick should start contracting immediately.

The test likely:
1. Sets ball radius to 60
2. Creates an ExpandingStrategy (expand = true by default)


3. Calls updateState once
4. Expects radius 58 (not 60)

With my current code, when radius is 60 and expand is true, I'm expanding to 62, hitting the cap, setting expand to false, but then clamping back to 60 without decrementing. The test expects that reaching the max should immediately flip the direction and contract in the same tick, so starting at 60 should result in 58 after one update.

The fix is to check the boundaries first, flip the direction if needed, then apply the movement in the new direction. So when I'm at 60 trying to expand, I detect the boundary, switch to contracting, and immediately subtract 2 to get 58. I'm verifying the test cases by tracing through the logic: expanding from radius 10 should increment by 2 each step, reaching 20 after 5 expansions, and contracting from radius 5 should trigger expansion again once the lower bound is hit. Since I can't see the exact test implementation, I'll stick with checking bounds first before applying the expansion or contraction logic.
One more fix needed for ExpandingStrategy ‚Äî when the ball is already at the max (60), it should immediately start contracting. I need to check bounds before adjusting.

blocks.text:
All 9 strategies implemented and all 36/36 tests passing. Here's a summary of each implementation:

| Strategy | Behavior |
|---|---|
| **StraightStrategy** | No-op (default move/bounce only) |
| **GravityStrategy** | `velocity.y += 1` each tick |
| **ColorStrategy** | Random RGB color each tick |
| **ReverseStrategy** | Negate velocity every 11 ticks |
| **ExpandingStrategy** | Oscillate radius ¬±2 between 5 and 60, checking bounds before adjusting |
| **TeleportingStrategy** | Random location every 21 ticks |
| **RandomWalkStrategy** | Velocity ¬±1 (exactly) each tick per axis |
| **ExplodingStrategy** | Spawn new ball on wall bounce, reset bounce flags |
| **SwitcherStrategy** | Delegate to internal strategy |

summary.resultText:
All 9 strategies implemented and all 36/36 tests passing. Here's a summary of each implementation:

| Strategy | Behavior |
|---|---|
| **StraightStrategy** | No-op (default move/bounce only) |
| **GravityStrategy** | `velocity.y += 1` each tick |
| **ColorStrategy** | Random RGB color each tick |
| **ReverseStrategy** | Negate velocity every 11 ticks |
| **ExpandingStrategy** | Oscillate radius ¬±2 between 5 and 60, checking bounds before adjusting |
| **TeleportingStrategy** | Random location every 21 ticks |
| **RandomWalkStrategy** | Velocity ¬±1 (exactly) each tick per axis |
| **ExplodingStrategy** | Spawn new ball on wall bounce, reset bounce flags |
| **SwitcherStrategy** | Delegate to internal strategy |
